name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
description: Raman spectra and composition data for titration experiments of various acids in aqueous solution. Includes acetic, citric, formic, itaconic, levulinic, oxalic, and succinic acids. Data for concentration monitoring and indirect hard modeling.
citeAs: @article{Echtermeyer_2020, title={Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species}, volume={75}, ISSN={1943-3530}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1177/0003702820973275}, DOI={10.1177/0003702820973275}, number={5}, journal={Applied Spectroscopy}, publisher={SAGE Publications}, author={Echtermeyer, Alexander and Marks, Caroline and Mitsos, Alexander and Viell, Jorn}, year={2020}, month=dec, pages={506--519}}
citation: Echtermeyer, Alexander Walter Wilhelm; Marks, Caroline; Mitsos, Alexander; Viell, Jörn. Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species. Applied Spectroscopy, 2021, 75(5):506–519. DOI: 10.1177/0003702820973275.

distribution.name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: Inline Raman spectra acquired during NaOH (1 mol L⁻¹) titration of citric acid (KMF Laborchemie, ≥99%) in deionized water (0.8 μS cm⁻¹) at 25 °C using a Kaiser RXN2 Raman analyzer (400 mW, 785 nm) with NIR fiber-optic immersion probe, spectral range 160–3285 cm⁻¹, 4 cm⁻¹ resolution, 60 s acquisition time. Ambient light excluded by non-transparent black PVC liner. pH monitored continuously with SenTix 940 electrode and Multil 3420 pH meter (WTW).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Standard Normal Variate (SNV) normalisation, linear baseline correction, and offset subtraction applied. Evaluation range 1025–1850 cm⁻¹, excluding atmospheric O₂ signal (1545–1565 cm⁻¹). Preprocessing performed in MATLAB R2017a (spectral subtraction) and PEAXACT ver. 4.5 (S-PACT).
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Mole fraction targets for four acid species (CA, CA⁻, CA²⁻, CA³⁻) derived from pH-based dissociation equilibria and Indirect Hard Modeling (IHM) using reported pKa values (method A). RMSECV ranges from 2.99 × 10⁻⁴ (CA) to 10.13 × 10⁻⁴ mol mol⁻¹ (water); accuracy is markedly lower for CA²⁻ (8.81 × 10⁻⁴) and CA³⁻ (7.20 × 10⁻⁴) due to the structural similarity of these two species.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: The high structural similarity of CA²⁻ and CA³⁻ makes their Pure Component Models (PCMs) nearly indistinguishable by IHM, causing systematic calibration errors at the higher dissociation stages. Ideal dilute mixture assumption applied despite activities deviating from unity at experimental concentrations. Data acquired at a single instrument (Kaiser RXN2, 785 nm) and temperature (25 °C).
rai:dataLimitations: Quantification accuracy is significantly reduced for CA²⁻ (R² = 0.9225, RMSECV = 8.81 × 10⁻⁴) and CA³⁻ (R² = 0.9154, RMSECV = 7.20 × 10⁻⁴) due to near-identical Raman signatures; not suitable for high-precision individual monitoring of higher dissociation stages without additional prior information. Models calibrated on NaOH titration in deionized water at 25 °C; ideal mixture assumption not valid above 0.1 mol L⁻¹.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Citric acid is widely used in food, pharmaceutical, and chemical industries, and is a major product of fungal fermentation. Accurate multi-stage dissociation monitoring enables better process control in pH-dependent extraction, crystallisation, and downstream purification, supporting efficient biobased production.
rai:dataUseCases: Process monitoring in triprotic acid systems; benchmarking IHM for structurally similar multi-species mixtures; evaluation of limits of spectroscopic species discrimination; PAT in citric acid production and purification processes.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on chemical samples including pure compounds, mixtures, and solutions. Preprocessing: Spectra may have undergone baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation, and wavenumber calibration. Exact steps are described in the accompanying publication. Annotation: Ground-truth target values measured by validated reference analytical methods (e.g., enzymatic assays, dynamic light scattering, gas chromatography, or DFT computation).
